arcs.title: The Third Fold
arcs.summary: Christine placed the survey notes on the map Thursday morning and the planning phase began in earnest. Steve's confidence has risen from seven to eight out of ten. Christine has identified which 1962 survey team was wrong but has not said so aloud. Orvell's postcard ('Soon,' postmarked locally) has arrived, and the brass duck and its coordinates sit beside the map. Day 4 should deliver the departure or the eve of it — the practical preparations, a final look at the coordinates, and the sense that the disputed ridgeline is about to be resolved by the only method that matters: going there.
